title: 10 best herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like ginger, turmeric, and willow bark contain compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in herbal teas such as green tea, ginkgo biloba, and rosehip help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by frozen shoulder, promoting healing and reducing scarring.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and peppermint contain compounds that help relax muscles, reduce tension, and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The Camellia sinensis extract, rich in catechins and theaflavins, helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder. It also promotes blood flow and relaxation of the muscles, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Regular application of the cream may also help to break down adhesions and scar tissue, contributing to a faster recovery and reduced symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, may help with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the condition's progression.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols, including epicatechin and epigallocatechin gallate, may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, which can help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

Catechins: Catechins, such as epigallocatechin gallate, may help with frozen shoulder by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and promoting tissue repair, ultimately leading to improved range of motion and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make creams for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Stems: Stems are used to make creams for frozen shoulder as they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Buds: Buds are used to make creams for frozen shoulder due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried camellia sinensis and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep the ground camellia sinensis in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the camellia sinensis infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the cooled camellia sinensis infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir well.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Some herbal extracts like turmeric (Curcuma longa), ginger (Zingiber officinale), and willow bark (Salix alba) have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving properties: Herbs like capsaicin (Capsicum annuum) from chili peppers, wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens), and menthol (Mentha piperita) can provide topical analgesia, helping to numb the affected area and reduce pain.

Wound healing and tissue repair properties: Certain herbs like aloe vera (Aloe barbadensis), tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia), and chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) may promote wound healing, reduce scarring, and enhance tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It contains capsaicin, a compound that blocks pain receptors, reducing discomfort and pain associated with frozen shoulder. The cream's warming effect also increases blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. Additionally, capsaicin's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and promote joint mobility, making it a natural and effective treatment option for individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a capsaicinoid found in Capsicum annuum, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and pain in the affected shoulder joint.

Catecholamines (e.g., dopamine, epinephrine): These catecholamines, which are a type of alkaloid, may help modulate the nervous system's response to pain, potentially leading to pain relief and improved range of motion in individuals with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are commonly used due to their high capsaicin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: The leaves may also be used, but their usage is less documented, possibly due to their lower capsaicin content compared to the fruits.
Seeds: The seeds may be used as well, possibly for their similar properties to the fruits and leaves, but more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and their color has changed to a deep red.
Dry the harvested fruits in a warm well-ventilated area for about 1 week or until they become brittle.
Grind the dried capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for about 5 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the ground capsicum annuum powder with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a smooth creamy herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This property has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help relieve pain and discomfort in frozen shoulder patients.

Saponins: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra root reduces inflammation and pain in the affected area. The cream's soothing and cooling effects also help to relax the muscles and improve range of motion. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra's antioxidant properties promote healing and reduce oxidative stress, leading to faster recovery from frozen shoulder. Its natural composition makes it an attractive alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and pain due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to improved shoulder mobility.

Licoricidin: This flavonoid glycoside exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation and reduced stiffness.

18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to improved shoulder mobility by reducing inflammation and pain, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: They are used because they contain various bioactive compounds that can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in frozen shoulder.
Barks: They are used due to their richness in glycosides and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Filter the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solids.
Mix 10-15 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter with 100 milliliters of the herbal extract in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Salicin content: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, helps reduce inflammation and pain in the affected joint, making it easier to move and alleviate stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, allowing for greater mobility and range of motion.

Pain-relieving properties: The combination of salicin and other compounds in white willow bark extract helps to numb the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it more manageable.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, salicin, mimics the effects of aspirin, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected joint. By decreasing inflammation, the cream allows for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. This helps to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable condition. Regular use may also aid in the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A glycoside compound that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Salicortin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint.

Triterpenoids: A group of compounds with anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation, modulate the immune response, and promote tissue repair in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Used because they contain salicylic acid, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain.
Stems: Used due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of salix alba bark, clean it thoroughly and dry it in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered bark with 20g of beeswax and 10g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and store in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced stiffness.
Antioxidant: Antioxidants in purple coneflower creams help protect the joint from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, work to reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to promote healing and tissue repair. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to improve range of motion and alleviate the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint, promoting healing and reducing pain.
Alkylamides: These compounds may help reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals, such as bradykinin, and promoting relaxation and reduced muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in inulin, which has anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating frozen shoulder.
Flowers: They contain alkylamides, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate frozen shoulder pain.
Leaves: They are a source of caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing frozen shoulder discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and roots from a trusted source.
Steep the dried echinacea mixture in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the echinacea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Blend 1/2 cup of the cooled echinacea liquid into the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.

Pain relief: The cream's analgesic and anti-pain compounds, such as helenin and sesquiterpenes, help alleviate the chronic pain and discomfort often experienced in frozen shoulder.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried arnica montana flowers and leaves from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Infuse the dried arnica montana in 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or until the oil turns a light yellow color.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and a small amount of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of stevia rebaudiana leaves and flowers from a mature plant in the morning.
Infuse the harvested stevia leaves and flowers in 1000 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused stevia liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter with the cooled stevia infusion in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture, approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Tumerones: These sesquiterpenoids have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, reduce inflammation, and improve joint mobility, making them beneficial for frozen shoulder treatment.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This curcuminoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are widely used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are used as they contain essential oils and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the ground curcuma longa powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the saucepan mixture.
Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory compound, allantoin, helps reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Symphytum officinale also contain mucilaginous properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in frozen shoulder sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers on a sunny morning for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 24 hours to prevent contamination.
Combine the dried plant material with 100g of carrier oil in a small saucepan and heat gently over low heat for 2 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 50g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax in a small saucepan and heat over low heat until the wax melts completely after 20 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dry artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steep 1 part of the harvested plant material in 5 parts of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the oil from plant material.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of the infused oil to create a smooth and stable cream base.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer or blender until it becomes light and fluffy like whipped cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce pain and stiffness. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, target the affected area, alleviating discomfort and promoting mobility. remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including vaccinium macrocarpon powder 250mg, coconut oil 50ml, beeswax 25g, and vitamin e oil 10 drops.
Combine powdered vaccinium macrocarpon 250mg with melted coconut oil 30ml in a double boiler stirring until smooth preparation time 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add beeswax 20g stirring until fully incorporated and cooled completely cooling time 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil 5 drops to the cooled mixture stirring well to combine and prepare for filling containers filling time 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store in a cool dark place shelf life 6 months or as indicated by expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joints. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves of about 10-12 inches long and free of blemishes.
Extract the gel from the inner part of the leaf using a spoon or a specialized tool.
Mix 20 grams of the extracted aloe vera gel with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f to 130°f.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken completely before transferring it to a sterilized glass jar for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of organic sunflower oil and steep for 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat gently over low temperature stirring constantly until melted completely.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars filling to the top and allowing it to cool before capping tightly.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa damascena flowers and sweet almond oil for a 10% extract ratio in a glass jar.
Steep 50g of dried rosa damascena flowers in 100ml of boiling distilled water for 15-20 minutes to create an herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup and discard solids within 30 seconds.
Mix the filtered herbal infusion with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup and heat gently for 1-2 hours at 70-80 degrees celsius.
Allow the infused cream to cool completely, then transfer it to sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that target the affected area, reducing pain and stiffness. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to alleviate inflammation and promote healing, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. Additionally, St John's Wort's analgesic properties help to numb the area, providing relief from discomfort and pain, making it an effective natural remedy for managing symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's Wort contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and reducing stiffness.
Analgesic: The herbal extract has analgesic properties that can help numb the pain caused by frozen shoulder, providing relief from discomfort and improving sleep quality.
Antioxidant: St. John's Wort is rich in antioxidants that can help protect against tissue damage and promote healing in the affected joint, reducing inflammation and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpenoids and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20g of fresh st john's wort (hypericum perforatum) leaves and flowers to obtain the dried herb.
Grind 10g of dried st john's wort into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release its active compounds.
Mix 5g of beeswax, 5g of coconut oil, and 1g of vitamin e oil with 20g of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl to create the cream base.
Add the powdered st john's wort to the cream base and stir until well combined, allowing it to steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The lemongrass oil in these creams has been shown to reduce pain and stiffness in affected joints, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. Additionally, the herbal extracts in the cream help to relax muscles and improve range of motion, making it easier to perform daily activities without discomfort or limitation. Overall, lemongrass creams offer a natural solution to alleviate symptoms associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, easing stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
Pain-relieving property: The cream's analgesic properties help block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic pain and stiffness often experienced with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant property: Lemongrass creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected joint, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots help to reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of lemongrass, rhyzomes contain analgesic properties that can ease stiffness and discomfort in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried, yielding one pound of greenery.
Chop the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves into small pieces and steep them in 500ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out as much juice as possible from the solids.
Mix the resulting herbal juice with 200g of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, heating the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the hot cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over several hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with frozen shoulder because they provide a soothing and cooling relief to the affected area. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles, reduces inflammation, and improves blood circulation, which can help reduce stiffness and pain. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint may help reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected joint. This natural remedy can be used externally or taken orally, making it a convenient and accessible treatment option for managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of prostaglandins.

Analgesic properties: The menthol and other active compounds in peppermint creams have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which can help alleviate the numbness, stiffness, and pain caused by frozen shoulder.

Vasodilatory effects: Peppermint creams can help improve blood flow to the affected area by dilating blood vessels, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots are effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve joint tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 100 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and warm to the touch.
Add 20 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine before pouring the mixture into sterilized jars.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before applying it topically to affected areas 3 times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and stiffness. The natural oils in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work to relax tense muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation. Additionally, fennel's warming effects help to dissolve adhesions and promote range of motion, making it easier to move the shoulder joint and alleviate the stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anesthetic properties of fennel, particularly its ability to stimulate the release of dopamine, can help alleviate pain and stiffness caused by frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and range of motion.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds can help protect against oxidative stress and reduce the risk of infection or further tissue damage in the affected joint, promoting a faster recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, providing relief from the stiffness and limited mobility of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground foeniculum vulgare flowers with 200 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup, stirring occasionally over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20 drops of lavender essential oil and 15 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine, then remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible, discarding the solids after 10 minutes of straining.
Allow the cream to mature in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily, before transferring it to a sterilized jar and refrigerating until use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with frozen shoulder because it targets the underlying inflammation and pain associated with this condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion root, combined with its natural analgesic qualities, work together to reduce stiffness and discomfort in the affected joint. Additionally, the cream's ability to promote blood flow and relaxation can help alleviate tension in the surrounding muscles, further reducing the symptoms of frozen shoulder. By addressing the root causes of pain and inflammation, dandelion creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, helping to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of sesquiterpenes, a type of compound found in dandelion, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing relief from chronic pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's high antioxidant content helps protect the affected tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: High in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as calcium and iron, dandelion leaves provide a soothing and nourishing topical treatment for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at a low temperature of 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate absorption and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herbal extract soothes and calms irritated joints, reducing stiffness and swelling that often accompanies this condition. Additionally, chamomile's natural antioxidant effects help to protect the joint from further damage and promote healthy tissue regeneration. By applying chamomile cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience improved range of motion, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced overall joint function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's constituents, including chamazulene, relax muscle spasms and reduce stiffness, which helps to ease pain and improve range of motion in frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an antioxidant that helps alleviate stiffness and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them properly for future use.
Mix 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for two weeks to allow for infusing and straining.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after one hour.
Whip 200 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the infused chamomile oil until it forms a smooth and creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for frozen shoulder are topical ointments infused with natural herbs and essential oils that help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by reduced mobility and stiffness in the shoulder joint. These creams, such as arnica and capsaicin, work by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, making it easier to move the shoulder. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved range of motion and reduced pain, allowing them to resume daily activities and enjoy a better quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal creams are conditions characterized by inflammation, stiffness, and limited mobility. These include arthralgia (joint pain), tendinitis (inflammation of tendons), and bursitis (inflammation of fluid-filled sacs). Herbal creams containing anti-inflammatory agents like capsaicin, arnica, and ginger can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and stiffness, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder is Turmeric and Ginger cream, combined with Arnica and Boswellia creams. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and swelling, while Ginger aids in improving blood circulation. Arnica cream accelerates the healing process by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen synthesis. Boswellia, also known as Frankincense, has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. These creams can be used in combination or alternated for optimal results.
